description: Let users switch between several related products or capabilities using a compact selector that drives one focused detail pane.
evidence_notes: Railway uses phase tabs to switch product narratives.
VoidZero uses a multi-product selector to spotlight one tool at a time.
Notion uses tabbed feature modules and carousel-like selectors for AI capabilities.
rules: Keep one focused pane active at a time so the user is not comparing everything at once.
Use short selector labels and concise supporting copy in the pane.
Make the active state obvious through contrast, underline, or surface changes.
implementation_notes: Implement with accessible tabs or segmented controls using proper roles and keyboard support.
Pair each pane with one clear CTA and one proof block.
Collapse large selector sets or convert them to swipeable rails on smaller screens.
avoid_when: The number of options is too high for quick comparison.
Every option requires a very different layout and breaks shell consistency.
The active pane lacks enough detail to justify the interaction.
